AI will make media and films more interesting: Suresh Chandra Menon
Updated
- April 13, 2026 12:51 am IST - CHENNAI
The Hindu Bureau
READ LATER
SEE ALL
Remove
Actor, writer and director Suresh Chandra Menon speaking at
The Hindu
EducationPlus, Career Counselling Fair '26 held in Chennai Trade Centre, Nandambakkam on Sunday.
                                          | Photo Credit: JOTHI RAMALINGAM B
Film maker, director and actor Suresh Chandra Menon on Sunday said that with the entry of AI, media and film making will become more interesting.
Speaking on the topic careers in media and entertainment at
The Hindu
EducationFair at the Chennai Trade Centre, Nandambakkam, he said that with the coming of AI, there would be more opportunities. On a question if film making can be taken over by AI, he said films are about creativity and emotions. AI will only become a tool in the hands of filmmakers. It will help cut costs,” he said.
Stating that technology had changed the media and film making, he recalled how he had started off as an assistant cinematographer. “All of us joined at the bottom and climbed our way up. Movies used to be shot on film, it used to take several days for us to see what we shot. It’s much easier to make a film today than during my times. Now they can be shot on an iPhone. Earlier you needed a producer with a lot of money. Now you have satellite, YouTube, OTT platforms and most recently, the vertical format. More new formats and new avenues will come,” said Mr. Menon who has worked on films including
Viduthalai
,
Vaazhve Mayam
,  and
Mangamma Sabadham
. The first film that he directed was
Puthiya Mugam
. ‘It was the first film shot in Sri Lanka after the riots,” he said.
“I believe that media and films are one of the best careers to choose. You meet new people daily; you create something new. Cinema gives you recognition, even beyond your lifetime. If you are 18 – 19 you don’t even have to wait to finish college, you can start making stuff on your phones. It’s never too early to be in the media. There are opportunities to make money on short films and documentaries. Cinema also pays technicians too. A successful cinematographer gets paid ₹2 crores.”
To a question on whether social media can be taken as a business, he said it can’t be taken as a business as such. “It is true that influencers are earning much. But to get 3-4 million followers is not easy. They have come to play a big role in advertising films. That has scope to generate income,” he explained.
Published
- April 13, 2026 12:50 am IST
